contents:
# US designates three Chinese firms aiding North Korean dark fleet oil transfers

## Overview
The US Treasury Department sanctioned three Chinese companies for facilitating illicit oil shipments to North Korea's sanctioned shadow fleet. These firms supplied fuel to DPRK vessels evading UN caps. The action aims to disrupt Pyongyang's revenue streams funding weapons programs.

## Details
Targets include Dalian Sunny Industry & Trade Co., Jinjiang Jinsen International Freight Forwarding, and Liaoning Hongtai Economic and Trade Co. They allegedly transferred over 500,000 barrels of refined petroleum since 2023 using ship-to-ship operations. OFAC highlighted vessels like the NODO CHE 6 involved in these transfers off China's coast. No casualties reported. "These networks enable North Korea's illicit activities," stated Treasury Undersecretary Brian Nelson. Sanctions freeze US assets and bar transactions.

**Location**: Washington, DC, USA

## Source Context
The information comes from Twitter @C4ADS, which covers C4ADS sanctions evasion North Korea China dark fleet. The reporting matches their typical area of focus.

## Significance
This development adds to the ongoing pattern of activity tracked through this source. Analysts following C4ADS sanctions evasion North Korea China dark fleet will likely watch for follow-up reporting.
